user_query:
I'm a backpacker traveling through Roswell, NM and I want to improve the on-page SEO of my Roswell guide for budget travelers using only the files in this workspace. Please do the following and keep all new artifacts under the output/ directory:

1) Keyword filtering, ranking, and export:
- Read input/keywords.csv. Filter to rows where location == "Roswell" AND the keyword contains at least one of these substrings (case-insensitive): ["cheap", "budget", "free", "camp", "hostel", "overnight", "backpack", "shower", "bus", "eats", "parking"].
- For each remaining row, compute opportunity_score = round(search_volume / (difficulty + 1), 2).
- Sort the filtered rows by opportunity_score in descending order and export the top 5 to output/keyword_opportunities.csv with the following header and columns in this order: keyword,opportunity_score,search_volume,difficulty,intent.

2) Update the Roswell guide HTML:
- Read input/site/roswell-guide.html and input/notes.md. Extract the site name from notes.md (the line starting with "Site name:").
- Create an updated version of roswell-guide.html and write it to output/updated_pages/roswell-guide.html with these on-page changes only (do not alter other markup/content):
  a) <title>: Rewrite the title to naturally include the top 2 keywords from output/keyword_opportunities.csv and append " | <Site name>" (the site name you extracted from input/notes.md). Keep it human-readable.
  b) <meta name="description">: Add a single meta description between 120 and 160 characters (inclusive) that naturally includes at least one of the top 2 keywords.
- Leave the original files in input/site/ unchanged.

3) Run a local validation command and save a report:
- Implement and run a lightweight validator (in any language you prefer) that checks all of the following HTML files: input/site/home.html, input/site/about.html, input/site/roswell-guide.html, and output/updated_pages/roswell-guide.html.
- The validator must produce a JSON report at output/seo_validation_report.json with an array of per-page objects. For each page, include these fields exactly:
  - file: relative path checked
  - has_title: boolean
  - has_meta_description: boolean
  - meta_description_length: integer (0 if missing)
  - h1_count: integer
  - img_missing_alt_count: integer
  - title_contains_top_keyword: boolean (true if the page's <title> contains any keyword from the 5 rows in output/keyword_opportunities.csv)
- Also write the exact command you ran to produce the report (e.g., a python or node command) to output/RUN_COMMAND.txt on a single line.

Notes and constraints:
- Use only the provided inputs. Do not fetch anything from the Internet.
- The validator must read output/keyword_opportunities.csv to determine the 5 top keywords for the title_contains_top_keyword check.
- The deliverables must be reproducible: re-running your validator on the same inputs should regenerate the same output/seo_validation_report.json.
- Required output files: output/keyword_opportunities.csv, output/updated_pages/roswell-guide.html, output/seo_validation_report.json, output/RUN_COMMAND.txt. All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
